compiled_state.title: extract_files() dir_roots in sprint-next-batch.sh should be config-driven, not hardcoded
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

The fix for 559d-b900 added 'plugins' to the hardcoded dir_roots set in extract_files(). But dir_roots is a mix of config values (cfg_src_dir, cfg_test_dir) and hardcoded strings ('app', '.claude', 'plugins'). A host project with a different top-level directory (e.g., lib/, packages/, services/) would need another code change to sprint-next-batch.sh.

## Fix needed

Add a config key (e.g., paths.extra_dir_roots) in dso-config.conf that extract_files() reads and merges into dir_roots. Remove all hardcoded directory names except those derived from existing config keys. Example config: paths.extra_dir_roots=plugins,app,.claude
